generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Deployed across the LEED Platinum Academic Building to collect real-time data on environmental conditions including temperature and humidity for energy efficiency and occupant comfort."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance AirQualitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Deployed across the LEED Platinum Academic Building to monitor CO2 levels and indoor air quality, integrated with the Digital Twin for sustainability validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Deployed in the University Library to track user presence and adjust environmental settings accordingly, improving energy efficiency through occupancy-aware optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance ParticulateMatterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Deployed in the University Library to measure PM2.5 and PM10 levels in real-time, supporting data-driven indoor optimization for occupant health and comfort."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ParticulateMatterDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Integrated into the Building Information Modeling (BIM) model to monitor structural and insulation systems for thermal performance and sustainability metrics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralPerformanceDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitored via the Digital Twin for energy efficiency and occupant comfort, providing real-time data on heating, ventilation, and air conditioning infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance AirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualitySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance ParticulateMatterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ParticulateMatterSensor
]

instance StructuralPerformanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralPerformanceSensor
]

instance HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPerformanceSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the physical LEED Platinum Academic Building, including structural, mechanical, and architectural elements, serving as the foundational data model for the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseBaSyxEnabler, iTwinJsEnabler
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time data on temperature, humidity, CO2 levels, and particulate matter (PM2.5/PM10) collected from sensors in the University Library for indoor climate control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted, AirQualityDataTransmitted, ParticulateMatterDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseBaSyxEnabler, iTwinJsEnabler
]

instance BuildingPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operational data from HVAC systems, building envelope, and structural performance sensors, used for continuous assessment of building performance against sustainability benchmarks."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted, StructuralPerformanceDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseBaSyxEnabler, iTwinJsEnabler
]

instance OccupancyPatternData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data tracking user presence and movement patterns in the University Library, enabling dynamic adjustment of environmental systems for energy efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseBaSyxEnabler, iTwinJsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseBaSyxEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source platform for Industry 4.0 applications, providing Asset Administration Shell (AAS) compliance, OPC UA integration, and robust connectivity framework for real-time data synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DynamicSimulationService, PerformanceAnalysisService, InteractiveVisualizationService, BidirectionalControlService,
                 IndoorEnvironmentMonitoringService, DynamicOptimizationService, RealTimeDashboardService
]

instance iTwinJsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "JavaScript library for developing infrastructure Digital Twin applications, enabling web-based development, collaboration tools, and spatial data integration for cross-platform accessibility."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService, RealTimeDashboardService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Frameworks and methodologies for Digital Twin development, including Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles for systematic development and maintainability."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedModelTransformationService, SemanticEnrichmentService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of building systems (e.g., HVAC, electrical, structural) and environmental parameters (e.g., temperature, humidity, air quality) via IoT sensors integrated with the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingSystemStateInsight, EnvironmentalParameterInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation of building performance under varying conditions (e.g., occupancy changes, weather variations) to predict energy consumption, thermal comfort, and system failures."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionPredictionInsight, ThermalComfortPredictionInsight, SystemFailurePredictionInsight
]

instance PerformanceAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of building performance against sustainability benchmarks (e.g., LEED criteria) and identification of optimization opportunities."
    DTDFVocab:provides SustainabilityBenchmarkInsight, OptimizationOpportunityInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D representations of the building and its systems, enabling stakeholders to visualize real-time data, historical trends, and simulation results."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataVisualizationInsight, HistoricalTrendVisualizationInsight, SimulationResultVisualizationInsight
]

instance BidirectionalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bidirectional communication between the Digital Twin and physical systems (e.g., HVAC, lighting) to automate adjustments based on predefined rules or optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACAdjustmentAction, LightingAdjustmentAction
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous tracking of indoor environmental parameters (e.g., temperature, humidity, CO2 levels, particulate matter) using a network of IoT sensors."
    DTDFVocab:provides IndoorEnvironmentStateInsight
]

instance DynamicOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of HVAC and lighting systems to maintain optimal conditions for occupant comfort and productivity, with minimal energy waste."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACOptimizationAction, LightingOptimizationAction
]

instance RealTimeDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Heatmaps and real-time dashboards displaying indoor environmental conditions, occupancy patterns, and system performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides IndoorEnvironmentHeatmapInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight, SystemPerformanceDashboardInsight
]

instance AutomatedModelTransformationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models to generate deployable Digital Twin artifacts with minimal manual intervention."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeployableDigitalTwinArtifactInsight
]

instance SemanticEnrichmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Definition of Building Control System (BCS) components (sensors, actuators, controllers) within BIM to create a semantic foundation for Digital Twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides SemanticallyEnrichedBIMInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingSystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time status of building systems including HVAC, electrical, and structural components, enabling proactive maintenance and operational adjustments."
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of environmental parameters such as temperature, humidity, and air quality for occupant comfort and sustainability validation."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for energy consumption under varying conditions, supporting optimization strategies for reduced energy use."
]

instance ThermalComfortPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-based predictions of thermal comfort levels for occupants, enabling adjustments to maintain optimal conditions."
]

instance SystemFailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Proactive identification of potential system failures through anomaly detection in sensor data and predictive analytics."
]

instance SustainabilityBenchmarkInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of building performance against LEED Platinum sustainability benchmarks, including energy efficiency and water conservation metrics."
]

instance OptimizationOpportunityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of opportunities for improving building performance, energy efficiency, and occupant comfort through data-driven recommendations."
]

instance RealTimeDataVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of real-time building data, enabling stakeholders to monitor operational states and system performance."
]

instance HistoricalTrendVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of historical performance trends, supporting long-term decision-making and strategic planning."
]

instance SimulationResultVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical display of simulation results, including energy consumption predictions and thermal comfort analyses."
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time status of indoor environmental parameters, including temperature, humidity, CO2 levels, and particulate matter for occupant health and comfort."
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentHeatmapInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Heatmap visualization of indoor environmental conditions, highlighting areas requiring attention for optimal occupant comfort."
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of occupancy patterns to inform dynamic adjustments of environmental systems for energy efficiency."
]

instance SystemPerformanceDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive dashboard displaying system performance metrics, occupancy data, and environmental conditions for real-time monitoring."
]

instance DeployableDigitalTwinArtifactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured, deployable artifacts generated from enriched BIM models, enabling seamless Digital Twin deployment and synchronization."
]

instance SemanticallyEnrichedBIMInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "BIM models enriched with semantic information about Building Control System components, supporting Digital Twin integration and operational control."
]

instance HVACAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of HVAC systems based on real-time data and predefined optimization rules."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LightingAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated modulation of lighting systems in response to occupancy patterns and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HVACOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Algorithmic recommendations for reducing HVAC energy consumption while maintaining occupant comfort, validated via simulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance LightingOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic optimization of lighting systems to minimize energy waste while ensuring adequate illumination for occupants."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator integrated with the Digital Twin for bidirectional control of heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems, enabling automated adjustments based on real-time data."
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for dynamic control of lighting systems in the University Library, responding to occupancy patterns and environmental conditions for energy efficiency."
]

instance BuildingEnvelopeController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller for structural and insulation systems, enabling adjustments to thermal performance and sustainability metrics based on Digital Twin analytics."
]

// C1: Environments/Components
instance LEEDPlatinumAcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Research-oriented academic building certified to LEED Platinum standards, optimized for energy efficiency, water conservation, and indoor air quality."
    base:contains BIMModel, HVACSystemsComponent, BuildingEnvelopeComponent, HVACActuator, BuildingEnvelopeController
]

instance UniversityLibraryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Indoor environmental monitoring system focused on climate control and occupant comfort optimization in the University Library."
    base:contains IndoorEnvironmentalData, OccupancyPatternData, HVACAndLightingSystemsComponent, OccupancySensor, LightingActuator
]

instance HVACSystemsComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning infrastructure monitored and controlled via the Digital Twin for energy efficiency and occupant comfort."
    base:isContainedIn LEEDPlatinumAcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance BuildingEnvelopeComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Structural and insulation systems analyzed for thermal performance and sustainability metrics through Digital Twin integration."
    base:isContainedIn LEEDPlatinumAcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance HVACAndLightingSystemsComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Dynamically controlled HVAC and lighting systems in the University Library, adjusted based on occupancy and external factors for optimal conditions."
    base:isContainedIn UniversityLibraryEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system supports **control flows from digital to physical** via the **DT Framework**, which acts as a central hub for issuing commands to physical infrastructure. Control messages are formatted to align with **Building Control Systems (BCS)** and are validated through the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring compatibility with deployed services. While specific command types (e.g., HVAC setpoints) are not mentioned, the system enables *Facility Managers* to interact with the DT for **real-time monitoring and control**, implying dynamic adjustments to lighting, HVAC, or other systems. **Feedback systems** are enabled via bidirectional data exchange, where physical responses (e.g., sensor confirmations) are relayed back to the DT for closed-loop verification. The framework does not explicitly describe **authentication mechanisms** or **conflict resolution**, but its **runtime configuration management** allows dynamic adjustments without redeployment, suggesting adaptive handling of control conflicts. Command execution confirmation is implied through the **continuous synchronization** loop between the DT and physical assets."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) or latency requirements are not quantified, the framework prioritizes **low-latency operational responsiveness** to support dynamic monitoring and control. The system leverages **runtime configuration management** to dynamically adjust synchronization granularity and service time scales without full redeployment, ensuring adaptability to varying operational demands (e.g., high-frequency sensor data for Building Control Engineers vs. periodic facility management analytics). The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **BIM-to-DT enrichment process**, where static BIM models are dynamically extended into operational Digital Twins, implying a transition from discrete (BIM) to continuous (real-time) time scales for real-world applications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, service-oriented constellation architecture**, structured around a **central DT Framework** that orchestrates interactions between **enriched BIM models**, **operational Digital Twins**, and **custom services**. Key orchestration features include:
  - **Bidirectional Data Flow**: The framework facilitates **real-time, bidirectional synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, ensuring **holistic life cycle management**.
  - **Modular Service Integration**: Custom services (e.g., analytics, control algorithms) are integrated via **standardized extension points**, enabling **dynamic runtime adjustments** without full redeployment.
  - **Role-Based Coordination**: The system defines **three primary roles**—**Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers**—each contributing to the Digital Twin life cycle through **specialized interactions** (e.g., device modeling, monitoring, service extension).
  - **Resource Management**: The **modular architecture** supports **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, with **low-overhead maintainability** ensuring efficient resource allocation.
  The constellation leverages **runtime configuration management** to adapt to evolving operational requirements, aligning with a **requirements-driven, evolutionary deployment model** (as depicted in Figure 1)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling & Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with semantic representations of Building Control Systems (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation & Artifact Generation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., executable models, service configurations) with **minimal manual intervention**. This ensures seamless transition from design to deployment.
  3. **DT Deployment & Synchronization**: The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, enabling continuous synchronization of system states. This phase aligns with **Facility Manager** operations, where monitoring and control actions are executed via the DT.
  4. **Custom Service Integration & Evolution**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. This phase supports **runtime evolution** of the DT to adapt to changing operational requirements, ensuring scalability and adaptability.
  Governance is implicit through **role-based access** (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and **structured use cases** (cf. Table II) that define stakeholder-specific responsibilities across the lifecycle. Transition management between phases is facilitated by **automated MDE pipelines**, reducing manual errors and ensuring consistency between physical and digital representations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The system emphasizes **closing the gap between design-time models and runtime operations** to ensure high fidelity in the Digital Twin’s representation of physical assets. Validation is supported through **semantic integration** and **real-time synchronization**, with an explicit focus on **quantifying the alignment** between the digital and physical domains. While no granular fidelity metrics (e.g., error thresholds or uncertainty quantification methods) are detailed, the framework includes mechanisms for **error detection and correction** via **continuous data validation** and **model-driven deployment processes**. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** is referenced as a key evaluative tool to assess how well the resulting DT reflects its physical counterpart, ensuring **quality assurance** throughout the lifecycle. The modular architecture further enables **extensible validation protocols** tailored to AECO-specific requirements."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with **standardized extension points** to support interoperability across heterogeneous systems. Communication protocols are explicitly defined by the integration of **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT data ingestion) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for digital twin state management), ensuring semantic consistency and real-time synchronization. The network topology is **centralized**, with the **DT Framework** acting as a hub for bidirectional data flows between BIM tools, physical assets, and operational services. While **bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, the system prioritizes **scalability** and **maintainability** through low-overhead updates. **Security protocols** are not detailed, but the framework’s reliance on standardized protocols (Hono/Ditto) implies adherence to industry best practices for secure communication. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not explicitly mentioned; instead, the focus is on **interoperability, scalability, and accessibility** for diverse user roles."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed via a **model-driven framework** grounded in Building Information Modeling (BIM), enabling **runtime configuration management** to adjust capabilities without full redeployment. The architecture supports **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, though specific hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise) are not detailed. Deployment is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools for device modeling, followed by **Facility Managers** and **DT Engineers** extending the framework’s capabilities. The system emphasizes **maintainability** through low-overhead updates and **accessibility** for users with varying technical expertise, but **availability and reliability assurance** are not explicitly addressed as non-functional requirements in the provided documents. The framework’s adaptability is enhanced by its **modular architecture**, allowing for incremental service integration."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework is **grounded in Building Information Modeling (BIM)** as its foundational standard, addressing BIM’s limitations in representing dynamic BCS components by enriching models with semantic metadata for sensors, actuators, and controllers. Standardization is further supported by **(1) a modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling interoperable integration of custom services and third-party tools; **(2) automated transformation pipelines** that adhere to MDE principles, ensuring consistency across deployment artifacts; and **(3) real-time data exchange protocols** (e.g., Eclipse Hono and Ditto) for live synchronization. While the document does not explicitly cite industry-specific standards (e.g., OPC UA, ISO 19650, or SysML), it acknowledges the **lack of standardized integration frameworks** in existing BIM-DT ecosystems and proposes the framework as a solution to bridge this gap. Compliance is implicit through the framework’s adherence to BIM’s data structures and the use of open-source technologies for extensibility."
]
